summary: Completed testing of Schedule A PDF Export feature. All backend API tests passed (7/7). All frontend UI tests passed. Export PDF button works correctly, generates valid PDF with styled document including trust name, date, asset tables by category, subtotals, and grand total.

issues: Console warning about <tr> inside <span> element - minor HTML nesting issue (inherited from previous iteration, doesn't affect functionality)

backend_api: PDF export returns valid base64 encoded PDF
Schedule A summary endpoint returns correct structure
Schedule A assets endpoint returns list of assets
frontend_ui: Export PDF button present with data-testid='export-pdf-btn'
Export PDF button has FileDown icon
Export PDF button enabled when assets exist
Toast notification 'Schedule A PDF downloaded' appears on success
Summary cards show: Total Assets (3), Total Value ($775,000), Categories (3)
3 category tables displayed: Real Property, Personal Property, Financial Accounts

seed_data_creation: Used demo data auto-seeding via 'Use Demo Data' button. Trust: Smith Family Trust with 3 assets worth $775,000
context_for_next_testing_agent: Schedule A PDF export feature is fully functional. PDF is generated using reportlab with navy/white color scheme. The PDF includes: header (SCHEDULE A), trust name, date prepared, total assets count, total value, asset tables grouped by category with subtotals, and grand total. Test trust 'Smith Family Trust' has 3 assets: Real Property ($450,000), Personal Property ($75,000), Financial Accounts ($250,000).

schedule_a_pdf_export.button_location: Schedule A page header, next to 'ADD ASSET' button

pdf_content.header: SCHEDULE A - Initial Corpus of the Trust
